policies.broad_gap: The backend does not invent a wall-clock or unmanaged receive-order throttling policy, so broader vendor-style update-rate semantics remain out of scope.
policies.supported_behavior: Update-rate reduction is implemented as logical-time-based throttling. Explicit and FOM-declared update-rate designators apply across direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions when there is a logical-time basis.
policies.title: Logical-time update-rate subset

policies.broad_gap: The backend does not model arbitrary custom transportation-type behavior beyond the reliable/best-effort subset.
policies.supported_behavior: Transportation semantics are implemented for the standard HLAreliable and HLAbestEffort pair, including FOM-defined defaults, explicit overrides, callback/query reporting, and restore persistence.

supported_subset_rows.notes: This canonical narrowed row captures the transport behavior the repo proves today: FOM-declared defaults, explicit reliable and best-effort override changes, distinct best-effort delivery semantics, query/report callback visibility, and restore persistence within the implemented transport subset.
supported_subset_rows.title: RTI shall use FOM-declared and explicitly selected reliable and best-effort transportation types for object updates and interactions, including query/report visibility and restore persistence within the implemented transport subset.
supported_subset_rows.vendor_notes: shared transportation-type and restore-persistence scenarios verify the supported Pitch transport subset and restore persistence

policies.title: Reliable and best-effort transport subset

policies.broad_gap: Message combination, packaging, and passelization are not explicitly modeled, so the permissive broad row stays partial by policy.
policies.supported_behavior: The backend preserves externally visible delivery semantics with direct unbatched callbacks.
policies.title: Unbatched callback delivery subset
